name: Devaughn Vele
headline: Efficient deep threat buried on the depth chart
body:
The numbers say Vele can play when given a route tree. Across nine games after the midseason move to New Orleans in 2025 he posted a 0.507 EPA per play and a 61.5% success rate, hauling in 25 of 39 looks for 293 yards and 2 scores. His 2024 Denver tape was even sharper: 0.638 EPA per play, a 74.5% catch rate on 55 targets, and 1.66 yards per route run. At 6-foot-5 he wins down the field, with 11-plus air yards per target and a 17.3 air yards per reception mark that screams vertical role.

The catch is opportunity. A 50.2% snap share and 36.8% route rate kept him from ever consolidating volume, and a season-ending shoulder injury on IR clouds his timeline. Projections land near 35-423-3, and his ADP sits in the WR97 range. He is a late flier whose efficiency profile only pays off if the Saints carve out real snaps.
